name: Carr v. Saul
question: Does a person seeking disability benefits under the Social Security Act forfeit their ability to challenge the appointment of an administrative law judge under the Appointments Clause if they fail to present that challenge during administrative proceedings?
description: A case in which the Court held that a person seeking disability benefits under the Social Security Act does not forfeit their ability to challenge the appointment of an administrative law judge if they fail to raise that challenge during administrative proceedings.
